title: ‘I stink at fantasy football’: Fan at Red Sox game goes viral with creative punishment
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: A fan at a Red Sox game displayed a sign reading 'I stink at fantasy football' as a punishment.
The fan sat alone in the upper deck of Kauffman Stadium, wearing bright clothing as part of the agreement.
NESN commentators remarked on the situation, noting the fan wasn't allowed to have a beverage during the game.

title: Rosebud in Somerville is closed, but just for renovations
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Rosebud Bar and Kitchen in Somerville temporarily closed for renovations, aims to reopen by mid-June.
Owner Koushik Koganti confirms updates include new floors, an HVAC system, and an updated menu.
Rosebud's current owners, Bindaas Restaurant Group, previously renovated when they acquired it in 2023.

title: ‘Infuriation’: Animal control reports rescuing puppy from 100-degree car at Legacy Place
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dedham Animal Control rescued a 5-month-old husky from a 100-degree car at Legacy Place.
The puppy was left in the vehicle while its owners shopped at the Dedham mall.
Dedham Animal Control did not specify potential charges or the puppy's current condition.

title: Widow of college police officer killed in crash sues Boston music venue, alleges they overserved other driver
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Laura Cole sues House of Blues Boston for overserving Keoma Duarte, alleging negligence in her husband's fatal crash.
Sgt. Jeremy Cole died in November 2024 after a wrong-way crash on I-95; Duarte faces manslaughter charges.
Lawsuit claims Duarte had a blood alcohol level of 0.19, visibly intoxicated at the House of Blues concert.

title: 3 must-see summer movies coming soon to a theater near you
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Disclosure Day', an alien film by Steven Spielberg, releases on June 12, inspired by his past sci-fi classics.
Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey', starring Matt Damon as Odysseus, premieres on July 17.
The Anthony Bourdain biopic 'Tony', filmed in Massachusetts, is set for an August release with exact date TBD.

title: LinkedIn releases top 10 best employers for career growth in Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: CVS Health topped LinkedIn's 2026 list for best career growth companies in Boston.
Healthcare, life sciences, and finance companies like Mass General Brigham and Fidelity Investments dominated the rankings.
Amazon ranked 10th, noted for AI engineering and mobile app development expertise.

title: Police looking to identify alleged Beacon Hill jewelry thief
author: John Waller
bullets: Boston police seek public's help identifying a woman accused of stealing earrings from Beacon Hill store.
The alleged theft occurred on May 4, near 126 Charles St., around 6 p.m., say officers.
Police released images of the suspect, who was seen leaving the store towards Boston Common.

title: MassDOT and Boston clash over Summer Street closure on World Cup game days
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: MassDOT plans to close a Summer Street section on World Cup game days due to anticipated foot traffic at South Station.
The City of Boston argues the closure uses eminent domain improperly and suggests alternative safety measures for the area.
MassDOT cites state law for right to close the street, with backing from Massachusetts State Police and MBTA safety staff.

title: Lucinda Williams on Bob Dylan, her stroke, and how this ‘World’s Gone Wrong’
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Lucinda Williams releases new protest album 'World's Gone Wrong' with influences from Bob Dylan.
Her album includes collaborations with artists like Norah Jones, highlighting socio-political themes.
Williams, a renowned songwriter, will perform three sold-out shows in Massachusetts this week.

title: What meteorologists are saying about scattered thunderstorms, when the heat will break in Boston
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston will experience scattered afternoon thunderstorms, mainly south of the MA Pike, with damaging wind gusts as a key threat.
Temperatures in Boston are nearing 90°F, but are expected to cool to seasonable levels by Thursday and Friday.
Potential for abnormally cold temperatures in Boston is forecasted for the upcoming weekend, according to the National Weather Service.
